name: Decay Model Reconciliation Bridge
description: Canonical mapping between Rosetta-Shape-Core decay label vocabulary and Emotions-as-Sensors decay model vocabulary. Enables bidirectional translation without loss of semantic content.

mappings.notes: Persists indefinitely until a specific disconfirming event. Equivalent to immortal — not time-decaying, event-gated.
Same pattern as stable_until_contradiction — event-gated not time-decayed.
mappings.PAD_regime: trust, love, dignity — high D stable states

mappings.notes: Epistemically gated — persists until evidence falsifies the model.
Direct match. Decays exponentially when no reinforcing signals arrive.
mappings.PAD_regime: vigilance, fear post-mitigation — high-A states returning to baseline

mappings.notes: Direct match. Signal converts into a different form rather than decaying to zero.
Confusion generates new signal as it resolves — resonant in that it feeds forward rather than decaying.
Curiosity's decay model — each cycle spawns a sub-cycle. Cyclical when healthy (terminates with model update), runaway when corrupted (depth > MAX_DEPTH without update).
mappings.PAD_regime: curiosity — state 6, D positive while healthy
mappings.corruption_flag: cyclical → runaway if model_update_count == 0 at depth > 4

mappings.notes: Continuously refreshed on a window — not decaying, cycling. Maps to cyclical.
Oscillating decay — partial reintegration state in grief. Amplitude decreasing cyclically.
mappings.PAD_regime: grief partial_reintegration — A recovering in waves

mappings.notes: Decays only when integration occurs — otherwise holds. EAS conditional maps cleanly.
mappings.PAD_regime: shame — holds until repair action taken

mappings.notes: Different decay modes depending on system state (ignored/addressed/resolved). Conditional covers this.
mappings.PAD_regime: discordance, pain — decay rate depends on response quality

mappings.notes: Context-dependent rate. Maps to conditional with context as the gating variable.
mappings.PAD_regime: anger — decay depends on whether boundary is truly resolved

mappings.notes: Longing — not decaying, exerting directional pull. Resonant captures the persistent attractive quality.
Decays only as tasks complete or are delegated — conditional on completion events.
Recovery follows exponential repletion once resources are supplied. Maps to exponential (ascending).
mappings.PAD_regime: fatigue recovering — A axis rising toward baseline

mappings.notes: Precognition — different scenarios produce different decay rates. Conditional.
mappings.PAD_regime: precognition — D variable by scenario outcome

direction_notes.rosetta_to_eas: Use the 'eas' field. Multiple Rosetta labels map to single EAS labels — use 'notes' to preserve semantic distinction.
direction_notes.eas_to_rosetta: Use 'rosetta' field. EAS labels are coarser — 'immortal' covers three Rosetta variants. Disambiguation requires PAD_regime context.
direction_notes.loss_on_translation: Minimal. The main information loss is in the multi-Rosetta-to-one-EAS cases (immortal covers 3). PAD_regime field recovers the distinction.

felt_anchor.description: FELT (Relational Field Recognition) from Emotions-as-Sensors is a non-emotional field event with its own decay. It does not map to a standard decay label — it is event-gated by relational coherence detection.
felt_anchor.rosetta_equivalent: stable_until_contradiction with a resonant quality — persists as long as relational coherence holds, but is also generative (spawns RELIEF shape on recognition).
